data.title: Southland's unit gets $60M wastewater treatment plant contract
data.description:
• Southland (NYSE:SLND) said its unit has been awarded a $60 million contract for the construction of a new wastewater treatment plant in the Southwest.
  • The range of work includes construction of new headworks, influent pump station, extended aeration basin, administration building, utility water station, and renovation of the crew building.
  • The firm said the award will be included in fourth quarter 2024 backlog.

data.title: Air Industries gets $4M contract
data.description:
• Air Industries (NYSE:AIRI) said it has received a follow-on contract valued at ~$4 million for arresting gear components for the F-35 Lightning II fighter aircraft.
  • The firm said the production of the arresting gear components is expected to begin in the first half of 2025.
  • The contract is slated for completion by year end 2025.

data.title: Activist investor renews campaign for Rio Tinto to quit London listing
data.description:
Activist investor Palliser Capital said Wednesday it wants Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) to scrap its primary London listing and unify its corporate structure in Australia.

In a letter to Rio's (RIO) board, the U.K.-based hedge fund said the miner's dual-listed structure "has proved to be an unmitigated failure for shareholders and requires urgent unification into a single Australian-domiciled holding company," adding shareholders have lost an estimated $50B in value due to the dual structure.

Rio (RIO) CEO Jakob Stausholm said in July that the company would not unify as an Australian company following a previous call by Palliser to do so; he said Rio's own review concluded changing the structure would destroy value for shareholders.

Palliser rejects that view: "Based on our extensive analysis, we believe that this readily actionable step would resolve the value destructive inefficiencies of the outdated DLC structure and present a path to $28B (27%) upside in the near term for PLC shareholders and further upside for the combined group over the medium term."

data.title: Dollarama reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Dollarama press release (OTCPK:DLMAF): Q3 sales increased by 5.7% to C$1,562.6 million, compared to C$1,477.7 million
  • This increase was driven by growth in the total number of stores over the past 12 months (from 1,541 stores on October 29, 2023, to 1,601 stores on October 27, 2024) and increased comparable store sales.
  • Comparable store sales increased by 3.3%, over and above 11.1% growth in the corresponding period of the previous year
  • EPS of C$0.98.
  • 18 net new stores opened, compared to 16 net new stores

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Foot Locker trims FY sales, profit outlook after Q3 miss
Chewy Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 misses by $0.03, revenue of $2.88B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Chewy press release (NYSE:CHWY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.20 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $2.88B (+4.8% Y/Y)  beats by $20M.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Hormel Foods reports FQ4 results, initiates FY25 guidance below estimates
SecureWorks Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 beats by $0.01, revenue of $82.7M
data.description:
• SecureWorks press release (NASDAQ:SCWX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $82.7M (-7.5% Y/Y).
  • Total annual recurring revenue grew to $288.8 million, an increase of 4% on a year-over-year basis.
  • As a result of the proposed transaction with Sophos, Secureworks is suspending financial guidance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025. As previously announced, Secureworks will not hold an earnings conference call for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025.

data.title: Foot Locker Non-GAAP EPS of $0.33 misses by $0.07, revenue of $1.96B misses by $40M
data.description:
• Foot Locker press release (NYSE:FL): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.33 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.96B (-1.5% Y/Y) misses by $40M.
  • Comparable sales increased by 2.4%, including global Foot Locker and Kids Foot Locker comparable sales growth of 2.8%. Notably, the Champs Sports and WSS banners saw positive comparable sales growth of 2.8% and 1.8%, respectively.
  • FY24 revenue consensus of $8.13B, EPS consensus of $1.3
